name: Block SSRF — Cloud Metadata and Internal IP Targeting
objective: Detect and block Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF) attempts targeting cloud metadata services (AWS IMDSv1, Azure IMDS, GCP metadata) and RFC-1918 internal IP ranges embedded in request parameters
risk_mitigated: SSRF allowing attackers to access AWS EC2 instance metadata (IAM credentials at 169.254.169.254), Azure IMDS, GCP metadata endpoint, or route requests to internal services not exposed to the internet
potential_side_effects: May block legitimate webhook tests that include internal URLs. May affect applications that intentionally proxy requests to metadata services (rare and should be redesigned). IP literal URLs (e.g., http://192.168.x.x) in webhook configuration UIs may be flagged.
tuning_notes: If your application legitimately receives internal IP addresses as parameters (e.g., a network monitoring tool), add specific endpoint exclusions. The metadata IP patterns (169.254.169.254, 100.100.100.200) are high-signal and should rarely need exclusions in web apps.
deployment_notes: SSRF is high severity — consider deploying directly in block mode after a 24h log-mode validation period. Monitor for any service-to-service patterns that include internal IPs.
monitoring_notes: Each SSRF block attempt is a high-priority signal. Alert on any single IP generating more than 5 SSRF blocks. Correlate with WAF and application logs for potential active exploitation.
cloudflare_expression: (http.request.uri.query contains "169.254.169.254" or http.request.uri.query contains "metadata.google.internal" or http.request.uri.query contains "169.254.170.2" or http.request.uri.query contains "100.100.100.200" or http.request.uri.query contains "localhost" or http.request.uri.query contains "127.0.0.1" or http.request.uri.query contains "[::1]" or http.request.body contains "169.254.169.254" or http.request.body contains "metadata.google.internal" or http.request.body contains "169.254.170.2")
